ORIGIN OF THE
RECALL

HISTORY RECORDS THE FACT
THAT TITS ESSENTIALLY AN
"A m e r c AN INSTITUTION,
tOLDER THAN OUR CONSTR
TUITION.

pages.raw_text:
sIn the recent consideration in
Spokane Of the amendment to the
city charter providing for the re-
call of elective officials, there were
those who commended that the re-
call was socialistic and antagonis-
tic to a representative form of gov
eminent


A recent investigation among
the records of the meetings of the
constitutional convention has dis
closed the fact that the recall is of
essentially American origin and is
older than our constitution itself.
A delegate to the convention from
Maryland, Luther Martin, pointed
to it's existence in his state at that
time and urged some such restric-
ton as to congressional represen-
tatives and United States senators.
Time has since shown that Dele
gate Martin had deeper insight
into the future than most of his
fellows.


In one of his debates, Martin
said:


"Mr. Speaker, in our state it is
provided by it's constitution that
the representatives in congress
shall be chosen annually, shall be
paid by the state, and shall be sub
ject to recall even within the year;
SO cautiously has our constitution
guarded against an abuse Of the
trust reposed in our representa-
tives in the federal government;
whereas, by the third and sixth
sections of the first article of this
new system, the senators are to
be chosen for six years instead of
being. chosen annually; instead of
being paid by their states who send
them, they, in conjunction with
the other branch, are to pay them
SELVES out of the treasury of the
uhi@d States, and are not liable
I'd De recalled during the period
for which they are chosen.


THUS, sir, for six years the SEN
at0fS are rendered totally and alb
solufkly Independent Of their
states, of whom they ought to be
the representatives, without any
bond or tie between them. During
that time they may join in meas
ures ruinous and destructive to
their states, even such as should
totally annihilate their state gov
ernments, and their states cannot
recall them, nor exercise any con-
troll over them"
